author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew IV. 12.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Now when Jesus had heard that John was delivered up, He departed into Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore doth He depart?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again instructing us not to go to meet temptations,but to give place and withdraw ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is no reproach, the not casting one’s self into danger, but the failing to stand manfully when fallen into it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To teach us this accordingly, and to soothe the envy of the Jews, He retires to Capernaum; at once fulfilling the prophecy,and making haste to catch the teachers of the world: for they, as you know, were abiding there, following their craft.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, how in every case when He is about to depart unto the Gentiles, He hath the occasion given Him by Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so in this instance, by plotting against His forerunner, and casting him into prison, they thrust out Christ into the Galilee of the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to show that He neither speaks of the Jewish nation by a part of it, nor signifies obscurely all the tribes; mark how the Prophet distinguishes that place, saying “The land of Nephthalim, by the way of the sea,beyond Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles, the people which sat in darkness, saw great light:”by darkness here not meaning that which is sensible, but men’s errors and ungodliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore he also added, “They which sat in the region and shadow of death, to them light is sprung up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that thou mightest learn that neither the light nor the darkness which he speaks of are sensible, in discoursing of the light, he called it not merely light, but “a great light” which elsewhere he expresses by the word, True:and in describing the darkness, he termed it, “a shadow of death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then implying that they did not of themselves seek and find, but that God showed Himself to them from above, he saith to them, “Light is sprung up;” that is, the light of itself sprang up and shone forth: it was not that they first ran to the light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth the condition of men was at the worst before Christ’s coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since they more than “walked in darkness;” they “sat in darkness;” a kind of sign that they did not even hope to be delivered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as persons not even knowing where to put a step forward, so they sat, overtaken by the darkness, not being able so much as to stand any more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “From that time Jesus began to preach and to say, Repent; for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “From that time:” what time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After John was cast into prison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did He not preach to them from the beginning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Indeed what occasion for John at all, when the witness of His works was proclaiming Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That hence also thou mightest learn His dignity; namely, that as the Fathers, so He too hath prophets; to which purpose Zacharias also spake; “And thou, child, shalt be called a prophet of the Highest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that he might leave no occasion to the shameless Jews; which motive He himself alleged, saying, “John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, he hath a devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wisdom is justified of her children.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And moreover it was necessary that what concerned Him should be spoken by another first and not by Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if even after both testimonies and demonstrations so many and so great, they said, “Thou bearest record of Thyself, Thy record is not true:”had He, without John’s saying anything, come into the midst, and first borne record Himself; what would they not have said?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause, neither did He preach before John, nor did He work miracles, until John was cast into prison; lest in this way the multitude should be divided.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also John did no miracle at all; that by this means also might give over the multitude to Jesus, His miracles drawing them unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, if even after so many divine precautions, John’s disciples, both before and after his imprisonment, were jealously disposed towards Him, and the people too suspected not Him but John to be the Christ; what would not the result have been, had none of these things taken place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause both Matthew distinctly notes, that “from that time He began to preach;” and when He began His preaching, He Himself also taught this same doctrine, which the other used to preach; and no word as yet concerning Himself doth the doctrine which he preached say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because it was for the time a great thing even for this to be received, forasmuch as they had not as yet the proper opinion about Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also at the beginning He puts nothing severe or grievous, as the other did, mentioning an axe, and a tree cut down; a fan, and a threshing-floor, and unquenchable fire; but His preludes are gracious: the Heavens and the kingdom there are the good tidings which he declares to His hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And walking by the sea of Galilee, He saw two brethren, Simon that was surnamed Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He saith unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you fishers of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they left their nets, and followed Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet John saith that they were called in another manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is evident that this was a second call; and from many things one may perceive this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there it is said, that they came to Him when “John was not yet cast into prison;” but here, after he was in confinement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And there Andrew calls Peter, but here Jesus calls both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And John saith, Jesus seeing Simon coming, saith, “Thou art Simon, the Son of Jona, thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, a stone.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Matthew saith that he was already called by that name; for his words are, “Seeing Simon that was called Peter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And from the place whence they were called, and from many other things, one may perceive this; and from their ready obedience, and abandonment of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For now they were well instructed beforehand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, in the other case, Andrew is seen coming into His house, and hearing many things; but here, having heard one bare word, they followed immediately.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since neither was it unnaturalfor them to follow Him at the beginning, and then leave Him again and return anew to their own craft, when they saw both John thrown into prison, and Himself departing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly you see that He finds them actually fishing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He neither forbad them at the first when minded to withdraw, nor having withdrawn themselves, did He let them go altogether; but He gave way when they started aside from Him, and comes again to win them back; which kind of thing is the great point in fishing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark both their faith, and their obedience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though they were in the midst of their work (and ye know how greedy a thing fishing is), when they heard His command, they delayed not, they procrastinated not, they said not, “let us return home, and converse with our kinsfolk,” but “they forsook all and followed,” even as Elisha did to Elijah.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because such is the obedience which Christ seeks of us, as that we delay not even a moment of time, though something absolutely most needful should vehemently press on us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also when some other had come unto Him, and was asking leave to bury his own father,not even this did He permit him to do; to signify that before all we ought to esteem the following of Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou should say, “the promise is very great;” even for this do I most admire them, for that when they had not as yet seen any sign, they believed in so great a reach of promise, and accounted all but second to that attendance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this, because they believed that by what words they were caught, by the same they would be able to catch others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To these, then, such was His promise: but to James and John He saith no such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the obedience of those that had gone before had by this time paved the way for these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides they had also heard many things before concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how he doth with exact care intimate unto us their poverty also: in that He found them sewing up their nets.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So exceeding great was their poverty, that they were mending what was worn out, not being able to buy others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this too was for the time no small proof of virtue, their beating poverty with ease, their supporting themselves by honest labor, their being bound one to another by the power of love, their having their father with them, and attending upon them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore He had caught them, then He begins in their presence to work miracles, by His deeds confirming the words of John concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He was continually frequenting their synagogues, even by this instructing them that He was not a sort of adversary of God and deceiver, but that He was come in accordance with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And while frequenting them, He did not preach only, but also showed forth miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this, because on every occasion, whensoever anything is done strange and surprising, and any polity is introduced, God is wont to work miracles as pledges of his power, which He affords to them that are to receive His laws.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, for instance, when He was about to make man, He created a whole world, and then gave him that law which he had in Paradise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when He was to give laws to Noah, He showed forth anew great miracles, in that He reduced again the whole creation to its elements,and made that fearful sea to prevail for a full year; and in that, amid so great a tempest, He preserved that righteous man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in the time of Abraham too He vouchsafed many signs; as his victory in the war, the plague upon Pharaoh, his deliverance from dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when about to legislate for the Jews, He showed forth those marvellous and great prodigies, and then gave the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Just so in this case also, being to introduce a certain high polity, and to tell them what they had never heard, by the display of the miracles He confirms what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus because the kingdom He was preach ing appeared not, by the things that appear, He makes it, though invisible, manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark the evangelist’s care to avoid superfluity of words;how he tells us not of every one of them that are healed, but in a few words speeds over showers of miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “they brought unto Him,” saith he, “all that were sick with divers diseases, and torments, and those which were possessed with devils, and those which were lunatic, and those that had the palsy, and He healed them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But our inquiry is this; why it can have been that He demanded faith of none of them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, what we find Him saying after this, “Believe ye that I am able to do this?”because He had not as yet given proof of His power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, the very act of approaching Him, and of bringing others to Him, exhibited no common faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they brought them even from far; whereas they would never have brought them, unless they had persuaded themselves of great things concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now then, let us too follow Him; for we also have many diseases of our soul, and these especially He would fain heal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since with this intent He corrects that other sort, that He may banish these out of our soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us therefore come unto Him, and let us ask nothing pertaining to this life, but rather remission of sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed He gives it even now, if we be in earnest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since as then “His fame went out into Syria,” so now into the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they indeed ran together on hearing that He healed persons possessed: and thou, after having much more and greater experience of His power, dost thou not rouse thyself and run?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But whereas they left both country, and friends, and kinsfolk; endurest thou not so much as to leave thy house for the sake of drawing near, and obtaining far greater things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather we do not require of thee so much as this, but leave thy evil habits only, and thou canst easily be made whole, remaining at home with thy friends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as it is, if we have any bodily ailment, we do and contrive everything to be rid of what pains us; but when our soul is indisposed, we delay, and draw back.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which cause neither from the other sort are we delivered: since the things that are indispensable are becoming to us secondary, and the secondary indispensable; and letting alone the fountain of our ills, we would fain cleanse out the streams.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that our bodily ills are caused by the wickedness of the soul, is shown both by him that had the palsy thirty and eight years, and by him that was let down through the roof, and by Cain also before these; and from many other things likewise one may perceive this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us do away then with the well-spring of our evils, and all the channels of our diseases will be stayed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the disease is not palsy only, but also our sin; and this more than that, by how much a soul is better than a body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us therefore now also draw nigh unto Him; let us entreat Him that He would brace our paralyzed soul, and leaving all things that pertain to this life, let us take account of the things spiritual only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or if thou cleave unto these also, yet think of them after the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither must thou think lightly of it, because thou hast no pain in sinning; rather on this very account most of all do thou lament, that thou feelest not the anguish of thine offenses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not because sin bites not, doth this come to pass, but because the offending soul is insensible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Regard with this view them that have a feeling of their own sins, how they wail more bitterly than such as are being cut, or burned; how many things they do, how many suffer, how greatly they mourn and lament, in order to be delivered from their evil conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They would not do any such thing, unless they were exceedingly pained in soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The best thing then is, to avoid sin in the first instance: the next to it, is to feel that we sin, and thoroughly amend ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if we have not this, how shall we pray to God, and ask forgiveness of our sins, we who take no account of these matters?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when thou thyself who hast offended art unwilling to know so much as this very fact, that thou hast sinned; for what manner of offenses will thou entreat God for pardon?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what thou knowest not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how wilt thou know the greatness of the benefit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Tell therefore thine offenses in particular, that thou mayest learn for what thou receivest forgiveness, that so thou mayest become grateful towards thy Benefactor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou, when it is a man whom thou hast provoked, entreatest friends, neighbors, and door-keepers, and spendest money, and consumest many days in visiting and petitioning, and though he that is provoked utterly reject thee once, twice, ten thousand times over, thou despondest not, but becoming more earnest thou makest the more entreaty; but when the God of all is provoked, we gape, and throw ourselves back, and live in luxury and in drunkenness, and do all things as usual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when shall we be able to propitiate Him? and how shall we by this very thing fail to provoke Him so much the more?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not so much sinning, as signing without even pain, causes in Him indignation and wrath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore it were meet after all this to sink into the very earth, and not so much as to behold this sun, nor to breathe at all, for that having so placable a Master, we provoke Him first, and then have no remorse for provoking Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet He assuredly, even when He is wroth, doeth not so as hating and turning away from us, but in order that in this way at least He may win us over to Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He continued after insult befriending thee, thou wouldest the more despise Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore in order that this may not be, He turns away for a little while, to have thee ever with Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us now, I pray you, take courage at His love to man, and let us show forth an anxious repentance, before the day come on, which permits us not to profit thereby.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as yet all depends on us, but then He that judges hath alone control over the sentence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Let us therefore come before His face with confession;”let us bewail, let us mourn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we should be able to prevail upon the Judge before the appointed day to forgive us our sins, then we need not so much as enter into the court; as on the other hand, if this be not done, He will hear us publicly in the presence of the world, and we shall no longer have any hope of pardon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no one of those who have not done away with their sins here, when he hath departed thither shall be able to escape his account for them; but as they who are taken out of these earthly prisons are brought in their chains to the place of judgment, even so all souls, when they have gone away hence bound with the manifold chains of their sins, are led to the awful judgment-seat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth our present life is nothing better than a prison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as when we have entered into that apartment, we see all bound with chains; so now if we withdraw ourselves from outward show, and enter into each man’s life, into each man’s soul, we shall see it bound with chains more grievous than iron: and this most especially if thou enter into the souls of them that are rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the more men have about them, so much the more are they bound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As therefore with regard to the prisoner, when thou seest him with irons on his back, on his hands, and often on his feet too, thou dost therefore most of all account him miserable; so also as to the rich man, when thou seest him encompassed with innumerable affairs, let him not be therefore rich, but rather for these very things wretched, in thine account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For together with these bonds, he hath a cruel jailor too, the wicked love of riches; which suffers him not to pass out of this prison, but provides for him thousands of fetters, and guards, and doors, and bolts; and when he hath cast him into the inner prison, persuades him even to feel pleasure in these bonds; that he may not find so much as any hope of deliverance from the evils which press on him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if in thought thou wert to lay open that man’s soul, thou wouldest see it not bound only, but squalid, and filthy, and teeming with vermin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no better than vermin are the pleasures of luxury, but even more abominable, and destroy the body more, together with the soul also; and upon the one and upon the other they bring ten thousand scourges of sickness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On account then of all these things let us entreat the Redeemer of our souls, that He would both burst asunder our bands, and remove this our cruel jailor, and having set us free from the burden of those iron chains, He would make our spirits lighter than any wing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as we entreat Him, so let us contribute our own part, earnestness, and consideration, and an excellent zeal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus we shall be able both in a short time to be freed from the evils which now oppress us, and to learn in what condition we were before, and to lay hold on the liberty which belongs to us; unto which God grant we may all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and power forever and ever.
